FULTON CENTER FOR REHABILITATION LLC

Overview

Address
2850 SPRINGDALE ROAD SW, ATLANTA, GA 30315
Phone

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.99 4.02 2.87 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.20 0.70 0.19 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 1.09 0.89 1.05 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.70 2.43 1.63 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.29 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.11 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.67
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.20
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.56
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
57.8%
annual
RN turnover
28.6%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.43
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
1.03
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-01 9 9 9 48 1 48
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-10-19 9 3 6 315 1 315
Total weighted health score
114.8
lower is better
Infection-control citations
0
last cycle

Deficiencies (23)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

18 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
4 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0628 Provide the required documentation or notification related to the resident's needs, appeal rights, or bed-hold policies. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0755 Provide pharmaceutical services to meet the needs of each resident and employ or obtain the services of a licensed pharmacist. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0759 Ensure medication error rates are not 5 percent or greater. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. F 2025-05-01 2025-06-15
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. J 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. J 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. J 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. G 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. J 2023-11-02 2023-12-01
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2023-10-19 2023-12-03
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-10-19 2023-12-03
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. D 2023-10-19 2023-12-03
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2022-08-26 2022-09-23
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2022-08-26 2022-09-23
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-08-26 2022-09-23
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2022-08-26 2022-09-23
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2022-08-26 2022-09-23
